model.label: Kimi K2.5
model.description: Moonshot AI • Reasoning model from the Kimi family built for complex thinking tasks.

pick.rationale: Despite the 8/9 seed line traditionally producing coin-flip outcomes, Utah State enters with superior efficiency metrics across the board, ranking 20th nationally in offensive rating and 70th in defensive rating compared to Villanova's 76th and 140th marks. The Aggies' 28-6 record includes both Mountain West regular season and tournament championships with momentum from defeating San Diego State in the title game. Villanova's significant loss of starting forward Matt Hodge to a season-ending ACL tear further tilts this matchup toward Utah State, which holds better SRS ratings and enters the tournament at full strength.

reasoningStep.title: Utah State Upset Over Villanova in 8/9 Matchup
reasoningStep.summary: Selected Utah State as the 9-seed upset winner over 8-seed Villanova based on superior offensive (20th vs 76th) and defensive (70th vs 140th) efficiency ratings, better SRS (17.36 vs 16.30), stronger record (28-6 vs 24-8), Mountain West championship momentum, and Villanova's critical injury to starting forward Matt Hodge (torn ACL). While 8/9 games are typically coin flips, Utah State's statistical advantages and health create a credible upset path.
evidence: Utah State: 28-6 record, SRS 17.36 (33rd), ORtg 120.0 (20th), DRtg 102.3 (70th), Mountain West Regular Season and Tournament Champions
Villanova: 24-8 record, SRS 16.30 (40th), ORtg 114.8 (76th), DRtg 105.3 (140th), 3rd in Big East
Villanova injury: Starting forward Matt Hodge (9.2 ppg, 3.6 rpg) suffered torn ACL, out for season
Utah State momentum: Defeated San Diego State 73-62 for MWC title, MJ Collins named Tournament MVP with 20 points

result.title: Villanova starting forward out for season with torn ACL
result.snippet: The players Matt Hodge A 6-foot-8 starting forward for the Villanova Wildcats who averaged 9.2 points and 3.6 rebounds per game before suffering a season-ending torn ACL injury .

result.title: Villanova loses starting forward Matt Hodge to devastating injury
result.snippet: Villanova's postseason hopes took a devastating turn just weeks before March Madness . Wildcats starting forward Matt Hodge suffered a torn ACL in his right knee during Saturday night's 89-57 ...

result.title: Hodge Suffered a Torn ACL in Right Knee Saturday - villanova.com
result.snippet: VILLANOVA , Pa. - Further testing has revealed that redshirt freshman forward Matt Hodge (Limburg, Belgium) suffered a torn ACL in his right knee during the second half of Saturday night's game at St. John's. The injury will require surgery and sideline Hodge for the remainder of the 2025-26 season.

modelTrace.content:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me summarize the key factors:

**Villanova (8 seed):**
- Record: 24-8 (15-5 in Big East, 3rd place)
- KenPom: 33rd, Torvik: 36th
- SRS: 16.30 (40th)
- ORtg: 114.8 (76th), DRtg: 105.3 (140th)
- **MAJOR INJURY:** Lost starting forward Matt Hodge (9.2 ppg, 3.6 rpg) to torn ACL
- Strength of schedule: 49th

**Utah State (9 seed):**
- Record: 28-6 (15-5 in Mountain West, 1st place)
- SRS: 17.36 (33rd) - HIGHER than Villanova
- ORtg: 120.0 (20th) - much better than Villanova
- DRtg: 102.3 (70th) - much better than Villanova
- Conference regular season champions
- Conference tournament champions (beat San Diego State 73-62)
- MJ Collins Tournament MVP
- Strength of schedule: 82nd (weaker than Villanova's)

**Analysis:**
- 8/9 games are typically very close, essentially coin flips
- Utah State has the better record (28-6 vs 24-8)
- Utah State has better SRS (17.36 vs 16.30)
- Utah State has significantly better offensive and defensive ratings
- Villanova has the injury to Matt Hodge, a starting forward
- Villanova has played a tougher schedule (49th vs 82nd)
- Villanova has Big East pedigree vs Utah State's Mountain West

Despite being the 8 seed vs 9 seed, Utah State actually looks like the better team on paper with better efficiency numbers and no injuries. The 8/9 line is often a coin flip and this looks like a case where the 9 seed is actually better.
 
The numbers favor Utah State—stronger efficiency metrics, better record, conference championship momentum, and no health concerns. Villanova's injury and weaker ratings make this an upset pick worth taking.

Now I have all the information needed to make an informed decision on this 8/9 matchup.

**Villanova Profile:**
- Record: 24-8 (15-5 Big East, 3rd place)
- Advanced metrics: KenPom 33rd, Torvik 36th, SRS 16.30 (40th)
- Offense: 114.8 ORtg (76th), Defense: 105.3 DRtg (140th)
- **Critical injury:** Starting forward Matt Hodge (9.2 ppg, 3.6 rpg) suffered a season-ending torn ACL in early March
- Strength of schedule: 49th

**Utah State Profile:**
- Record: 28-6 (15-5 Mountain West, 1st place)
- SRS: 17.36 (33rd) - actually higher than Villanova
- Offense: 120.0 ORtg (20th) - significantly better than Villanova
- Defense: 102.3 DRtg (70th) - significantly better than Villanova
- Mountain West Regular Season and Tournament Champions (beat San Diego State 73-62)
- Tournament MVP: MJ Collins (20 points in championship)
- Strength of schedule: 82nd (weaker than Villanova)

This is a compelling upset opportunity. Despite the traditional 8/9 seed line being a coin flip, Utah State enters with superior efficiency metrics across the board (ranking 50+ spots better on offense and 70 spots better on defense), a better record, conference championship momentum, and no injury concerns. While Villanova has the Big East pedigree and stronger schedule, the loss of starting forward Matt Hodge to a torn ACL significantly weakens their frontcourt depth. In a matchup of evenly-seeded teams, Utah State's better health and superior statistical profile provide a credible path to victory.
